_comment: T190/T197: non-regression coverage ratchet baseline, enforced by scripts/check-coverage-ratchet.sh in CI's coverage-report job. Numbers are LINE coverage percentages from reportgenerator's merged TextSummary. Backend: all four coverlet Cobertura reports merged, collected with `--settings backend/coverlet.runsettings` (T197) so EF migrations/Designer/ModelSnapshot files, obj/ generated build artifacts, and the Seeder tool assembly are excluded from the denominator. Frontend: flutter test --coverage's lcov.info, still unfiltered (see frontend.note). Raise these values only via a deliberate, standalone edit once coverage genuinely improves; never lower them.
_t197History: T197 (2026-07-19): re-measured backend UNFILTERED as of this commit at 20.2% (up from the stored 19.2% 2026-07-18 baseline, purely from this session's new test code — not a re-baselining decision on its own). Then measured FILTERED (backend/coverlet.runsettings applied) at 74.4% on the exact same test run. The backend.lineCoveragePercent below is the FILTERED figure, now that CI collects with the settings file — this is a genuine methodology change, not a coverage improvement, and is not comparable to prior unfiltered numbers. ExcludeByAttribute (GeneratedCodeAttribute/CompilerGeneratedAttribute) was tested and rejected — see backend/coverlet.runsettings' own comment for why (it silently drops whole files of genuine record/primary-constructor-based production code in this codebase, confirmed by isolating each filter and comparing assembly/class/file counts). Fail-closed zero-coverable-lines behavior was re-verified (not assumed unchanged): forcibly excluded everything and confirmed scripts/check-coverage-ratchet.sh still hard-fails (exit 1, 'treating as inconclusive, not a genuine 0%').

backend.note: T197 filtered baseline (backend/coverlet.runsettings applied): dotnet test across all four backend/tests/* suites (Acceptance 51, Contract 39, Integration 1, Unit 36) with --collect:"XPlat Code Coverage" --settings backend/coverlet.runsettings, merged via reportgenerator. 3229/4339 coverable lines covered, 37 assemblies. Unfiltered equivalent on the same run was 20.2% (3320/16368) — see _t197History.

frontend.note: Phase 4 exit / Phase 5 entry baseline, NOT re-measured by T197 — left as-is deliberately. The generated Dart API client (frontend/packages/api_client) is a separate pub package `flutter test --coverage` run from frontend/app never touches, so there is no equivalent generated-client dilution to filter here; frontend's own generated l10n/drift files remain included/unfiltered exactly as before. Measured locally: flutter test --coverage (87-case suite as of 2026-07-18; the suite has since grown to 94 in this session's own work, so this number is stale on that count alone, independent of the filtering question T197 was scoped to). 1895/2399 coverable lines covered.
